title: She’s running the Boston Marathon for her former patient with Morquio syndrome
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Erin Donnelly, a Boston-based physical therapist, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for her former patient with Morquio syndrome.
Her patient, Giovanni Thompson, impacted her deeply before passing away at age 5 after being discharged from Franciscan Children's.
Giovanni's mantra, 'I can do hard things,' inspired Donnelly to participate in the marathon in his honor.

title: Bruins fall to Devils 4-3 in OT as US Olympic hero Jack Hughes has 3 assists
author: The Associated Press
bullets: The Bruins lost 4-3 in overtime to the Devils, with Paul Cotter scoring the game-winning goal.
Jack Hughes assisted three goals, reaching 402 career points in 414 regular-season games for the Devils.
The Bruins remain two points ahead of Columbus, clinging to the final Eastern Conference wild-card playoff spot.

title: Tyngsborough man charged with murder after human remains found buried under garage floor, police say
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Shawn Sullivan, 40, charged with murder after human remains found under his garage in Tyngsborough, police say.
Remains believed to be Jill T. Kloppenburg, missing from Lowell since January 2025, officials announce.
Sullivan to be arraigned on murder, assault, and improper disposal of remains charges in Lowell court Tuesday.

title: Why a Texas mom is running the Boston Marathon for her son — and his MGH doctor
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Adrienne Felterman, 45, from Houston, will run the 2026 Boston Marathon to honor her son Charles and his doctor.
Charles, her son, has Dup15q syndrome, and Dr. Ronald Thibert at MGH has been pivotal in his epilepsy treatment.
Felterman aims to raise funds for Massachusetts General Hospital, emphasizing gratitude for the exceptional care received.

title: ‘She is truly a superhero’: Why a Belmont athlete is running Boston for his wife
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Boston Marathon "I’m running for her and so many people like her.
To show them that there is always a finish line.
" Ben Flowers is running the 2026 Boston Marathon.

title: She’s running the 2026 Boston Marathon to support kids with cancer
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Kathleen Labrie is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for the Massachusetts General Hospital Pediatric Cancer team.
Labrie's participation honors children with cancer, allowing donations to honor individuals via the I Run For Them (IRFT) motto.
Her personal connection to pediatric cancer led her to run for various honorees with unique stories and challenges.

title: Noah Kahan announces premiere date for documentary coming to Netflix
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: Noah Kahan's documentary 'Noah Kahan: Out of Body' premieres on Netflix on April 13.
The documentary covers Kahan's success with 'Stick Season,' his mental health, and Vermont roots.
Kahan's new album, 'The Great Divide,' will be released less than two weeks later on April 24.

title: Jon Keller, longtime WBZ political analyst, returns to TV
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Jon Keller returns to WBZ-TV as a 'special contributor' after being laid off last fall due to layoffs.
Keller will resume political analysis and moderate debates; 'Keller At Large' returns Sunday with Gov. Maura Healey.
Keller, a 12-time Emmy winner, joined WBZ in 2005 after 14 years at WLVI-TV, continuing his MASSterList column.

title: ‘A relic of a bygone era’: Here’s why readers want Massachusetts to stop changing its clocks
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Massachusetts lawmakers consider ending daylight saving time changes with a proposed shift to permanent Atlantic Standard Time.
A poll showed 83% of 470 Boston.com readers favor ending the biannual clock change, citing disruptions to routines and health.
The proposed bill suggests adopting permanent time changes only if other New England states take similar measures.

title: Impaired Stoneham teen drove 118 mph, fled police, and then crashed, N.H. authorities say
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: 18-year-old from Stoneham arrested for driving 118 mph and fleeing police on I-95 in Greenland, NH.
Teen failed to stop for Maine State Police and crashed into a wall in Stratham, NH.
Charged with felony reckless conduct and misdemeanor DUI; held without bail after receiving minor injuries.
